body.title: TWM self-eviction — decide policy when newly-inserted row loses to all existing rows
body.result: retired: superseded by T-twm-salience-time-decay. SELF_EVICTED WARNING already shipped in 088c9bf5 (observability unblocked). No fold-in needed — with time-decay, old rows naturally lose eviction ranking, making the policy question (protect-newest / threshold-on-admit) unnecessary.
body.description:
Observed live 2026-04-23: Cortex.twm_push silently drops newly-inserted rows when TWM (50 slots) is full of high-salience unintegrated observations. INSERT succeeds, eviction runs, new row is the lowest-priority tie-breaker → it gets picked for deletion. Caller sees a valid obs_id return but the observation never lands. Surfaced via pr_* / intent_decay / relationship_drift test failures.

**Current state (commit 088c9bf5):** SELF_EVICTED WARNING now fires at the push site with full context. Observability is unblocked. Behavior unchanged — silent drop becomes logged drop.

**Design question (this ticket's scope):** what should the actual policy be? Options:
1. Status quo + warning (done) — accept that TWM is attention-bounded and sometimes a new low-priority observation doesn't win.
2. Protect-newest — eviction never picks the row just inserted in the same call. Existing rows lose first; if no existing row can be evicted (e.g. all higher priority than new one), drop the new one explicitly with a distinct return (-2?).
3. Salience-threshold on admit — if new row's salience is below the current 50th-percentile, reject at the door (don't insert) and return a clearly-not-obs-id value.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/memory/cortex.py (twm_push eviction block ~line 4189-4220), tests/test_twm_eviction_policy.py (new). Possibly call sites that rely on obs_id semantics.

**Design rules:** docs-in-code, test-plan-or-why-not, oop-first. No new memory schemas.

**Scope boundary:** IN — pick policy (1/2/3 above or hybrid), implement, tests that verify the chosen policy holds. OUT — background sleep-based eviction of stale TWM rows (that's T-twm-sleep-eviction, separate ticket).

**Test plan:** unit tests on twm_push with pre-filled TWM: verify push of below-floor salience behaves per chosen policy (drops with named return / evicts an existing row / etc). Integration: observe in prod that SELF_EVICTED warnings disappear after the fix is deployed.
